events.title: Valdosta wastewater overflow spills millions of gallons into Spring Branch and the Withlacoochee River
events.description: Valdosta officials reported an overflow from an equalization basin at the Withlacoochee WPCP that reached Spring Branch and the Withlacoochee River. The report referenced a total spill volume of about 6.7 million gallons, including about 1.34 million gallons of raw sewage, following heavy rainfall.

sources.headline: Valdosta spilled 6.7 million gallons including 1.34 million gallons raw sewage 2024-04-12
News 2024

key_quotes.quote: Valdosta spilled 6.7 million gallons including 1.34 million gallons raw sewage

events.title: Houston 69th Street Wastewater Treatment Plant spill releases over 100,000 gallons after rainfall and equipment failure
events.description: Houston Public Works reported an accidental domestic wastewater spill at the 69th Street Wastewater Treatment Plant after intense rainfall led to equipment failure. The spill affected areas within the plant and Buffalo Bayou, and the city advised private-well users in a specified area to boil or distill water.

sources.headline: Wastewater spill at 69th Street Wastewater Treatment Plant
Houston wastewater spill at 69th Street treatment plant after rainfall (report)

key_quotes.quote: Wastewater spill at 69th Street Wastewater Treatment Plant

events.title: Charleston Harbor wastewater spill releases nearly 10,000 gallons after mechanical failure at Plum Island plant
events.description: Reporting said the Charleston Water System acknowledged nearly 10,000 gallons of wastewater discharged into Charleston Harbor after a mechanical failure. A broken valve was cited, and bacterial testing and public health cautions for nearby waters were reported.

sources.headline: Nearly 10k gallons wastewater spilled into Charleston Harbor

key_quotes.quote: Nearly 10k gallons wastewater spilled into Charleston Harbor

events.title: NOAA: April 2024 ranked 12th-warmest April for the contiguous United States
events.description: NOAA reported the contiguous U.S. average April temperature was 53.8°F (2.7°F above average), ranking as the 12th warmest April in the 130-year record. Above-average warmth covered much of the country, with regional exceptions noted in the report.

sources.headline: U.S. Climate Summary: April 2024

key_quotes.quote: contiguous U.S. average temperature was 53.8°F, 2.7°F above average, ranking 12th warmest

events.title: East Africa: March–April rains drove extensive flooding with fatalities and displacement reported
events.description: FEWS NET’s April 30 seasonal monitor described heavy April rains contributing to extensive flooding in parts of East Africa. The monitor reported fatalities, displacement, elevated river levels, and ongoing flood risk in several basins across multiple countries.

sources.headline: East Africa Seasonal Monitor: April 2024

key_quotes.quote: heavy April rains ... causing extensive flooding, fatalities, and displacement

events.title: Pakistan: unusually heavy April rains associated with fatalities and housing damage
events.description: An April 17 report described unusually heavy April rains in Pakistan, with deaths linked to lightning, flash floods, and collapsed structures. The report also noted damage to homes and cited official remarks that April rainfall was nearly twice the historical average.

sources.headline: Lightning, downpours kill in Pakistan as April rainfall nearly twice historical average

key_quotes.quote: April rainfall was nearly twice the historical average

events.title: United Arab Emirates: record heavy rainfall caused widespread flooding and disruption
events.description: Severe rains on April 16–17 caused widespread flooding in the United Arab Emirates, disrupting transport and infrastructure across multiple emirates. Reporting described the event as the heaviest rainfall in decades, with major impacts in Dubai and northern emirates.

sources.headline: Desert city of Dubai floods as heaviest rainfall in 75 years hits UAE

key_quotes.quote: heaviest rainfall in 75 years hits UAE

events.title: M7+ earthquake near Hualien, Taiwan, triggers landslides and infrastructure disruption
events.description: A strong earthquake struck near Hualien City, Taiwan (April 2 UTC / April 3 local time). Reporting and official earthquake information indicated widespread impacts including landslides and disruption to transportation and utilities.

location.name: Near Hualien City, Hualien County, Taiwan

sources.headline: M 7.4 - 18 km SSE of Hualien City, Taiwan
Taiwan shaken by strongest earthquake in 25 years, building collapses

key_quotes.quote: M 7.4 - 18 km SSE of Hualien City, Taiwan

events.title: Explosive eruptions at Mount Ruang (Indonesia) send ash and tephra onto the island
events.description: Explosive activity at Indonesia’s Ruang volcano in mid-April produced ash and volcanic debris affecting the small island. Satellite observations described ash coverage and landscape changes over April 12–20.

sources.headline: A Blanket of Ash from Ruang

key_quotes.quote: A Blanket of Ash from Ruang

events.title: Dam collapse in Orsk amid Ural River flooding prompts evacuations
events.description: Reuters reported that a dam in Orsk, Russia, failed during severe flooding in the Ural River basin. The incident led to evacuations and widespread inundation in the area.

sources.headline: Russia declares federal emergency after Ural river floods

key_quotes.quote: Russia declares federal emergency after Ural river floods

dedupe.cluster_hint: Orsk dam failure / Ural River floods April 2024

events.title: Little Yamsay Fire ignites in Klamath County, Oregon
events.description: The Little Yamsay Fire incident was reported for Klamath County in southern Oregon. The incident began during April 2024 and continued beyond April based on the incident listing.

sources.headline: Little Yamsay Fire

events.title: NOAA satellite narrative reports widespread smoke over central and southeastern U.S.
events.description: NOAA Satellite Services Division reported light-to-moderate density smoke associated with seasonal fire activity and small wildfires across a broad swath of the central and southeastern United States, with transport extending into southern Canada. The narrative describes regional smoke conditions over April 256, 2024.

sources.headline: Smoke data narrative 2024D260109

events.title: NOAA satellite narrative reports smoke transport from suspected wildfire in Chihuahua into the U.S.
events.description: NOAA Satellite Services Division described light-to-medium density smoke attributed to a suspected wildfire in Chihuahua, Mexico. The smoke was reported moving northeast into the U.S., including Texas and New Mexico, and spreading downwind across multiple states during April 26, 2024.

sources.headline: Smoke data narrative 2024D261734

dedupe.cluster_hint: NOAA smoke narrative Chihuahua to US 2024-04-26

events.title: NOAA confirms fourth global coral bleaching event
events.description: NOAA confirmed a fourth global coral bleaching event in April 2024, citing widespread coral heat stress affecting reef areas across multiple ocean basins. The announcement indicates elevated ecological risk for coral reef ecosystems at a global scale.

sources.headline: NOAA confirms fourth global coral bleaching event

key_quotes.quote: NOAA confirms fourth global coral bleaching event

events.title: Study detects emerging contaminants in Grand Canyon South Rim springs
events.description: USGS and the National Park Service reported detections of emerging contaminants, including PFAS and pharmaceutical residues, in spring waters on the South Rim of Grand Canyon National Park. The findings raise concerns about potential chronic exposure risks for sensitive spring ecosystems and possible links to wastewater pathways.

location.name: Grand Canyon National Park (South Rim springs), Arizona, USA

sources.headline: USGS and National Park Service study detects emerging contaminants in Grand Canyon South Rim springs
Emerging contaminants in Grand Canyon South Rim springs

key_quotes.quote: study detects emerging contaminants ... Grand Canyon South Rim springs

dedupe.cluster_hint: emerging contaminants detected in Grand Canyon South Rim springs

events.title: U.S. EPA finalizes stronger Clean Air Act standards for chemical plants (ethylene oxide, chloroprene)
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency issued a final rule strengthening Clean Air Act standards for certain chemical plants. The rule targets emissions of ethylene oxide and chloroprene, citing the goal of reducing associated cancer risks for nearby communities.

sources.headline: Biden-Harris Administration Finalizes Stronger Clean Air Standards for Chemical Plants
Final Rule to Strengthen Standards for Synthetic Organic Chemical Manufacturing Industry (SOCMI) and Other Source Categories

key_quotes.quote: finalizes stronger clean air standards for chemical plants

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA final rule chemical plants ethylene oxide chloroprene April 2024

events.title: U.S. EPA finalizes suite of standards to reduce pollution from fossil fuel-fired power plants
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency announced multiple final rules intended to reduce pollution from fossil fuel-fired power plants. The announcement describes actions addressing climate, air, water, and land pollution under several statutory authorities.

sources.headline: Biden-Harris Administration Finalizes Suite of Standards to Reduce Pollution from Fossil Fuel-Fired Power Plants

key_quotes.quote: finalizes suite of standards to reduce pollution from fossil fuel-fired power plants

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA power plants final rules April 2024

events.title: UNEP plastics treaty negotiations (INC-4) open in Ottawa
events.description: UNEP reported the opening of the fourth session of the Intergovernmental Negotiating Committee (INC-4) on plastic pollution in Ottawa. The meeting was described as a penultimate negotiating session toward an international legally binding instrument on plastic pollution, including in the marine environment.

sources.headline: INC-4 opens in Ottawa to advance plastics treaty negotiations

key_quotes.quote: opening of INC-4 in Ottawa on April 23, 2024

events.title: U.S. EPA announces settlement with Sasol Chemicals over alleged chemical accident prevention violations
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency reported a finalized settlement with Sasol Chemicals regarding alleged Clean Air Act chemical accident prevention violations at its Westlake facility. EPA said the settlement requires payments exceeding $1.4 million and corrective actions, tied in part to an October 2022 fire and other compliance findings.

sources.headline: EPA Announces $1.4M Settlement with Sasol Chemicals for Alleged Chemical Accident Prevention Violations

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA settlement Sasol Westlake Louisiana April 2024
